mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate revenues, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in monthly retail sales and accelerating consumer price indices indicates robust aggregate demand that outpaces supply constraints, forcing equity valuations higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative 20-day forward return in > 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and business investment, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during expansionary phases, with a t-statistic below 1.5 on the coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding a 2% annualized pace confirms sustained economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth remains below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns are uncorrelated with GDP growth rates.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts signals synchronized strength in labor markets and residential construction, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered months, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of restrictive monetary policy indicates supply-side bottlenecks that constrain corporate output, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles fail to predict negative 5-day returns, with a t-statistic above -1.5 on the coefficient.
